mappings.rationaleAI: Business as organized profit-seeking activity covering legal structures, taxation, and commercial regulation addresses laws governing economic transactions (D), commercial transactions (D.2), and law of business associations (D.3).

Corporations as legally distinct entities with shareholders and limited liability directly address corporate companies (D.3.a.ii), while governance separation and capital structure connect to the structure of corporate finance (D.3.c).
Law broadly covers private law including property (A), contract, property, and tort (C), commercial and business law (D), and civil procedure (E).

Property rights encompassing private, public, and collective ownership address law of property (A), historical development of property rights (A.1), types of ownership (A.3), and intangible/incorporeal property including intellectual property (A.5).
Family as a social unit with marriage, kinship, divorce, and children's rights directly addresses family law (B) and laws governing the institution of the family (B.1).
Marriage as a legally recognized union establishing rights and obligations between spouses directly addresses laws concerning the marriage contract (B.1.a).
Banks as financial intermediaries accepting deposits and extending loans under fractional-reserve banking connect to law of commercial transactions (D.2).
Finance's concern with monetary resources, valuation, and capital allocation connects to law of commercial transactions (D.2) and the structure of corporate finance (D.3.c).
Insurance as a risk-management mechanism based on contractual agreements connects to law of contracts (D.1) and commercial transactions (D.2).
Privacy as controlling personal information connects to injuries to personality including invasions of privacy and defamation (C.5.a).

Home as human residence with tenure types (owner-occupancy, rental) connects to real and personal property (A.4), temporal division of property rights including freehold and leasehold estates (A.6), and law of mortgages (A.8).
Buildings as permanently enclosed structures serving occupancy functions connect to laws concerning tangible property and the distinction between real and personal property (A.4).
Trade as the transfer of goods and services through market networks connects to law of commercial transactions including sales of goods (D.2).
Work spanning paid employment, labor rights, and automation debates connects to labour law (D.4).

Parenting's provision of support to children by biological parents, guardians, or state institutions connects to laws concerning children including legitimacy, adoption, guardianship, and parental obligations (B.1.b).
Children as legal minors with issues of health, education, labor, and protection connect to laws concerning children including legitimacy, adoption, and parental rights (B.1.b).
Trade unions' collective bargaining for wages, benefits, and working conditions directly address labour law (D.4).
Money as legal tender and medium of exchange connects to transfer of negotiable instruments including promissory notes and checks (D.2.a.ii).
Slavery as coerced labor with contemporary forced labor and human trafficking connects to labour law governing working conditions and prohibitions on exploitation (D.4).
